name: Castillete de Fayón — circular from Nonaspe
name_original: Castillete de Fayón — circular desde Nonaspe
region_intro: Hiking around Nonaspe, Spain, offers diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Ebro River, featuring riverine landscapes and agricultural areas. Trails often traverse gentle hills and valleys, providing varied elevation changes suitable for different fitness levels. The natural environment around Nonaspe includes open fields and some forested sections, making it a suitable destination for beginner hikes Nonaspe and more experienced hikers. Best hiking trails around Nonaspe The most popular hiking route is From Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca to Puente y túnel de la antigua línea de tren, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route follows a scenic path along the river, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillete de Fayón — circular from Nonaspe, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.5 km) path. This challenging hike features significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the area. Local hikers also love the Old Church Tower of Fayón – La Mineta loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Nonaspe is defined by river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and gentle hills. The network offers options for easy hikes Nonaspe as well as more challenging routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Nonaspe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Nonaspe?
A: Nonaspe offers a selection of 7 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from riverine paths to gentle hills.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, Nonaspe has 1 easy hiking trail perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. The region's terrain, characterized by gentle hills and river valleys, is generally suitable for less experienced hikers.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Nonaspe?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Nonaspe features 1 difficult trail. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979544644/castillete-de-fayon-circular-from-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillete de Fayón — circular from Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.5 km long and includes significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on hikes around Nonaspe?
A: Hiking around Nonaspe offers diverse landscapes, primarily defined by its proximity to the Ebro River. You'll encounter scenic riverine paths, agricultural areas, open fields, and gentle hills. Some routes also feature forested sections, providing varied scenery throughout your walk.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979544644/castillete-de-fayon-circular-from-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillete de Fayón — circular from Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging loop with panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón – La Mineta loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate option through varied terrain.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Nonaspe?
A: Many trails in Nonaspe offer views of the Ebro River and its surrounding landscapes. You can also explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mineta&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6243829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarraña-Algars Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable point of interest.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979544644/castillete-de-fayon-circular-from-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillete de Fayón — circular from Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic vistas. Additionally, you can find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4601307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro River at Mequinenza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6446129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Riba Roja Reservoir and the Pilar Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Nonaspe?
A: The hiking trails in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic river paths, the varied terrain, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs near Nonaspe for recreation?
A: Yes, the region is close to significant bodies of water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the Mequinenza Reservoir (often referred to as the 'Sea of Aragon') are prominent features, offering scenic views and opportunities to enjoy the riverside environment.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Nonaspe?
A: The duration of hikes in Nonaspe varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168864091/from-faio-la-pobla-de-massaluca-to-puente-y-tunel-de-la-antigua-linea-de-tren" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca to Puente y túnel de la antigua línea de tren&lt;/a&gt; (13.4 km), typically take around 3 hours 45 minutes, while shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón – La Mineta loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; (9.2 km) can be completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Are there any historical sites to visit while hiking in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, the area around Nonaspe is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mineta&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are featured on local hiking loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4613070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Mausoleum of Fabara&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical attraction nearby.
tips: Nice picture from the path of the Algás river as it passes through Nonaspe.

Located at the top of the hill, about seven kilometers from the town of Nonaspe, we find this building that was built as a watchtower during the third Carlist war in the 19th century to dominate the Matarraña valley.
At this point is the monument to the "Bombers of the Generalitat de Catalunya" who lost their lives in the fire of September 14, 1994.

waypoints.name: Monument in Memory of the Firefighters of the Generalitat de Catalunya (1994 Fire)
